name: env-continuity-bloat-v2
description: Per-seed baseline of continuity_block_chars on Turn 1 using a fixed, env-rich founding premise. Same town-main-street scenario across many seeds. Tighter warning thresholds than env-continuity-dedup-v2 because the founding prompt is fixed and variance should be low. Regression signal: if continuity_block_chars grows significantly across seeds with identical input, that indicates env-fact injection is not deterministic or the dedup pass is seed-sensitive. post_extract intentionally omitted: computeMetrics reads continuity_block_chars from the primary /narrate response via the response fallback path.

request_template.action: I am standing on Main Street in a quiet town, in front of a row of weathered buildings with cracked pavement and a red-and-white awning. A rusted mailbox stands crooked nearby.
request_template.WORLD_PROMPT: A quiet town on a warm afternoon. Main Street has cracked pavement, faded facades with peeling paint, a red-and-white awning over a shop door, and a rusted mailbox with its flag at half-mast. Weeds push through the concrete seams.
